segments.en: Is there any possibility, do you think, that we never get to this thing called AGI?

segments.zh: 我们如何区分 AGI 和超级智能这个术语？

segments.en: What's the difference?
segments.zh: 有什么区别？

segments.en: Yeah, so the difference is that AGI is a more vague and weak term.
segments.zh: 是的，所以区别在于 AGI 是一个更模糊、更弱的术语。

segments.en: I know super intelligence is a bit more precisely defined.
segments.zh: 我知道超级智能的定义更精确一些。

segments.en: It's better than the best humans at everything faster and cheaper.
segments.zh: 它在所有方面都比最优秀的人类更好，而且速度更快、成本更低。

segments.en: AGI is more like, it stands for artificial general intelligence,
segments.zh: AGI更像是，它代表通用人工智能，

segments.en: which means AI is a can do things in general rather than like some specific
segments.zh: 这意味着人工智能可以做一般性的事情，而不是像某些特定的事情

segments.en: task. And so arguably we've already achieved AGI, right?
segments.zh: 任务。所以可以说我们已经实现了通用人工智能，对吧？

segments.en: If you use cloud code or something like that, it's like it can do a lot of stuff.
segments.zh: 如果您使用云代码或类似的东西，它就像可以做很多事情。

segments.en: It's almost kind of like a little employee that you can like have go do stuff.
segments.zh: 它几乎有点像一个小员工，你可以让它去做一些事情。

segments.en: So it is quite general.
segments.zh: 所以说还是很一般的。

segments.en: It's not maximally general though.
segments.zh: 但它并不是最通用的。

segments.en: It can't do everything.
segments.zh: 它不能做所有事情。

segments.en: Whereas super intelligence by definition
segments.zh: 而超级智能的定义

segments.en: can do all the things that a human can do but better.
segments.zh: 可以做人类能做的所有事情，而且做得更好。

segments.en: at the moment.
segments.zh: 此刻。

segments.en: There are some real world things that humans can still do
segments.zh: 人类仍然可以做一些现实世界的事情

segments.en: because these AI's are still stuck on my computer.
segments.zh: 因为这些人工智能仍然卡在我的电脑上。

segments.en: The way that people talk about this is that they
segments.zh: 人们谈论这个问题的方式是

segments.en: basically just say we've achieved super intelligence
segments.zh: 基本上只是说我们已经实现了超级智能

segments.en: for cognitive tasks.
segments.zh: 用于认知任务。

segments.en: Then you can talk about like full super intelligence
segments.zh: 然后你就可以谈论像完整的超级智能

segments.en: that can do the physical stuff.
segments.zh: 可以做物理上的事情。

segments.en: And are we gonna get there?
segments.zh: 我们能到达那里吗？

segments.en: are we gonna get there with both?
segments.zh: 我们两个都能到达那里吗？

segments.en: I think so.
segments.zh: 我想是的。

segments.en: I mean, again, this is not something
segments.zh: 我的意思是，再说一次，这不是什么

segments.en: that we can be certain about.
segments.zh: 我们可以确定。

segments.en: You asked like, is it possible we'll never get there?
segments.zh: 你问，我们有可能永远无法到达那里吗？

segments.en: Yes, it's possible we'll never get there.
segments.zh: 是的，我们可能永远无法到达那里。

segments.en: I don't think it's likely though.
segments.zh: 但我认为这不太可能。

segments.en: I think that there's nothing sort of like magical
segments.zh: 我认为没有什么比魔法更神奇的了

segments.en: about the human brain.
segments.zh: 关于人类的大脑。

segments.en: It's just a bunch of neurons.
segments.zh: 它只是一堆神经元。

segments.en: It is possible for a digital system
segments.zh: 对于数字系统来说是可能的

segments.en: to do similar functions in the same way
segments.zh: 以同样的方式完成类似的功能

segments.en: that like a plane can fly just like a bird,
segments.zh: 就像飞机一样可以像鸟一样飞翔，

segments.en: not in the same way as a bird necessarily.
segments.zh: 不一定与鸟一样。

segments.en: It doesn't have, it's not flying in the same way
segments.zh: 它没有，它不以同样的方式飞行

segments.en: that a bird flies, but it flies, you know?
segments.zh: 鸟儿会飞，但它会飞，你知道吗？

segments.en: So it does seem like, yeah, like, seems possible.
segments.zh: 所以这看起来确实是可能的。

segments.en: You've written all these, you know, these research reports.
segments.zh: 你知道，这些研究报告都是你写的。

segments.en: You're working on another one that'll be released
segments.zh: 您正在开发另一款即将发布的产品

segments.en: likely on the 9th of July.
segments.zh: 可能是7月9日。

segments.zh: 您曾在 OpenAI 内部工作过。

segments.en: You then quit OpenAI because you were concerned
segments.zh: 然后你退出了 OpenAI，因为你担心

segments.en: about what was going on there
segments.zh: 关于那里发生的事情

segments.en: and about the future of the industry.
segments.zh: 以及该行业的未来。

segments.zh: 你知道的比我多。

segments.en: Are you optimistic about the future or pessimist?
segments.zh: 您对未来持乐观态度还是悲观态度？

segments.en: Are we heading to a bad place if things don't change based on everything that you know?
segments.zh: 如果事情不根据你所知道的一切进行改变，我们会走向一个糟糕的地方吗？

segments.en: I think we are headed to a bad place if things don't change.
segments.zh: 我认为如果事情不改变的话我们将会走向一个糟糕的地方。

segments.zh: 我对此没有信心。

segments.zh: 我会说大约70%。

segments.en: It's very, very hard to predict, of course.
segments.zh: 当然，这非常非常难以预测。

segments.en: But yeah, it seems like the current default path is heading towards a very, very scary
segments.zh: 但是，是的，当前的默认路径似乎正在走向非常非常可怕的道路

segments.en: place.
segments.zh: 地方。

segments.en: How do you contend with that personally and emotionally?
segments.zh: 您个人和情感上如何应对？

segments.en: It's rough.
segments.zh: 很粗糙。

segments.en: I mean, I think it's the sort of thing that like gets me down on a regular basis, but also
segments.zh: 我的意思是，我认为这是一种经常让我沮丧的事情，但也

segments.en: I've been dealing with this for so many years now that I've sort of gotten used to it.
segments.zh: 我已经处理这个问题很多年了，现在我已经习惯了。

segments.en: If that makes sense. Yeah, I'll put it this way. I would be incredibly happy if all my predictions
segments.zh: 如果这是有道理的话。是的，我会这样说。如果我所有的预测

segments.en: turn out to be wrong and AI hits the wall, for example.
segments.zh: 例如，结果证明是错误的，人工智能碰壁了。

segments.en: It gets you down on a regular basis.
segments.zh: 它经常让你情绪低落。

segments.en: I used to be known as a pretty chipper and optimistic person, but in 2020,
segments.zh: 我曾经被认为是一个非常开朗和乐观的人，但在2020年，

segments.en: my AI timelines predictions started collapsing due to GPT-3 and the scaling laws papers
segments.zh: 由于 GPT-3 和缩放定律论文，我的 AI 时间线预测开始崩溃

segments.en: and the bio-inkers report, which I can talk about if you're interested.
segments.zh: 还有生物墨水家的报告，如果你感兴趣的话我可以谈谈。

segments.en: But basically, some events happened in 2020 that convinced me that actually this stuff
segments.zh: 但基本上，2020 年发生的一些事件让我相信，实际上这些东西

segments.en: was quite plausibly coming by the end of the decade.
segments.zh: 到本世纪末，这一目标很有可能实现。

segments.en: And humanity is very obviously not ready for this in a whole bunch of different ways.
segments.zh: 很明显，人类在很多方面还没有为此做好准备。

segments.en: And so that's obviously very scary.
segments.zh: 这显然非常可怕。

segments.en: And that's extremely scary well because of all the things you've said.
segments.zh: 由于你所说的所有事情，这非常可怕。

segments.en: but again, because of this recursive self-improvement
segments.zh: 但同样，由于这种递归的自我完善

segments.en: where AI's can train themselves.
segments.zh: 人工智能可以自我训练。

segments.en: And at such point, we're starting to lose hold
segments.zh: 在这一点上，我们开始失去控制

segments.en: of what's going on here.
segments.zh: 这里发生的事情。

segments.en: I mean, the AI's are already training themselves to be clear.
segments.zh: 我的意思是，人工智能已经在训练自己变得清晰。

segments.en: It's more like closing the entire research loop, right?
segments.zh: 这更像是关闭整个研究循环，对吧？

segments.en: So doing everything.
segments.zh: 所以凡事都做。

segments.en: Yeah, like right now, a lot of the training data
segments.zh: 是的，就像现在一样，大量的训练数据

segments.en: is generated by AI's.
segments.zh: 是由AI生成的。

segments.en: A lot of the reinforcement, like the grading that happens,
segments.zh: 很多强化，比如发生的评分，

segments.en: doling out of positive and negative reinforcement
segments.zh: 正强化和负强化的分配

segments.en: is itself done by AI's.
segments.zh: 本身是由AI完成的。

segments.en: Can you explain that in layman's terms further?
segments.zh: 您能用通俗的语言进一步解释一下吗？

segments.en: Yeah, so an important thing for everybody to understand
segments.zh: 是的，每个人都必须明白这一点很重要

segments.en: is that modern AI systems are not software in a normal sense.
segments.zh: 现代人工智能系统并不是普通意义上的软件。

segments.en: I mean, they are technically software,
segments.zh: 我的意思是，它们在技术上是软件，

segments.en: but they're not lines of code, you know?
segments.zh: 但它们不是代码行，你知道吗？

segments.en: It's not like some engineers at Anthropic went
segments.zh: 这不像 Anthropic 的一些工程师那样

segments.en: and wrote lines of code that basically says like,
segments.zh: 并编写了几行代码，基本上是这样的，

segments.en: you know, when the user asks for this type of thing,
segments.zh: 你知道，当用户请求这类事情时，

segments.en: then go do this type of thing for this many steps or whatever.
segments.zh: 然后去做这种类型的事情这么多步骤或其他什么。

segments.en: There's nothing like that.
segments.zh: 没有那样的事。

segments.en: Instead, it's a neural net, you know?
segments.zh: 相反，它是一个神经网络，你知道吗？

segments.en: What's that?
segments.zh: 那是什么？

segments.en: Well, think about how the brain is a bunch of neurons
segments.zh: 好吧，想想大脑是如何由一堆神经元组成的

segments.en: connected to each other.
segments.zh: 彼此相连。

segments.en: Yeah. That are firing signals back and forth.
segments.zh: 是的。那是来回发射信号。

segments.en: The brain learns over time.
segments.zh: 大脑会随着时间的推移而学习。

segments.en: The types of patterns of firing that caused success,
segments.zh: 导致成功的射击模式类型，

segments.en: that caused a dopamine rush
segments.zh: 导致多巴胺激增

segments.en: or various other types of feedback,
segments.zh: 或各种其他类型的反馈，

segments.en: get reinforced and fire more often.
segments.zh: 得到增援并更频繁地开火。

segments.en: And the types of patterns that caused failure
segments.zh: 以及导致失败的模式类型

segments.en: like touching a hot stove,
segments.zh: 就像触摸热炉一样，

segments.en: get anti-reinforced, get destroyed
segments.zh: 得到反强化，被摧毁

segments.en: so that they fire less often.
segments.zh: 这样他们就可以减少开火的次数。

segments.en: And as a result of all of that,
segments.zh: 由于这一切，

segments.en: you over the course of years,
segments.zh: 你这些年来，

segments.en: learn to act in the world.
segments.zh: 学会在世界上行动。

segments.en: And you learn all sorts of skills, and you learn world models,
segments.zh: 你学习各种各样的技能，你学习世界模型，

segments.en: you learn beliefs about the world,
segments.zh: 你学习关于世界的信念，

segments.en: and you can sort of mentally stimulate how it's going
segments.zh: 你可以在精神上刺激事情的进展

segments.en: and stuff like that.
segments.zh: 诸如此类的事情。

segments.en: So artificial neural nets are like that, except artificial.
segments.zh: 所以人工神经网络就像那样，只不过是人工的。

segments.en: So it starts off as a giant, tangled spaghetti
segments.zh: 所以它一开始就是一根巨大的、纠结的意大利面条

segments.en: mess of randomly generated artificial connections
segments.zh: 随机生成的人工连接的混乱

segments.en: called parameters.
segments.zh: 称为参数。

segments.en: These days, they might be something like 10 trillion
segments.zh: 如今，它们可能约为 10 万亿美元

segments.en: parameters in the biggest AIs.
segments.zh: 最大人工智能中的参数。

segments.en: So it starts off randomly generated.
segments.zh: 所以它一开始是随机生成的。

segments.en: So it's, of course, completely useless.
segments.zh: 所以它当然是完全没用的。

segments.en: Like if you give it some input, it'll just produce gibberish
segments.zh: 就像如果你给它一些输入，它只会产生乱码

segments.en: as an output.
segments.zh: 作为输出。

segments.en: But then they train it.
segments.zh: 但随后他们会训练它。

segments.en: And they start with pre-training, which
segments.zh: 他们从预训练开始，

segments.en: is where you give it a bunch of internet text,
segments.zh: 就是你给它一堆互联网文本的地方，

segments.en: and you show it the first piece of text,
segments.zh: 然后你向它展示第一段文字，

segments.en: and you put that in as the input.
segments.zh: 然后你把它作为输入。

segments.en: And then it gives a gibberish output.
segments.zh: 然后它给出了一个乱码的输出。

segments.en: And then you positively or negatively
segments.zh: 然后你积极或消极地

segments.en: reinforced it based on how accurate that output was at predicting the next piece of text.
segments.zh: 根据输出预测下一段文本的准确性来强化它。

segments.en: So, it's basically playing this game of like predict the next word.
segments.zh: 所以，它基本上是在玩这个预测下一个单词的游戏。

segments.en: Isn't that how it happens with babies?
segments.zh: 婴儿身上不都是这样吗？

segments.en: I think I had a neuroscientist tell me that babies have more neural connections than adults.
segments.zh: 我想一位神经科学家告诉我，婴儿比成人有更多的神经连接。

segments.en: And yeah, so toddlers have twice as many neural connections as adults.
segments.zh: 是的，幼儿的神经连接数量是成年人的两倍。

segments.en: And they, I guess they whittled down through reinforcement.
segments.zh: 我想他们是通过加固来削弱的。

segments.en: We have more pathways when we're younger.
segments.zh: 当我们年轻的时候，我们有更多的途径。

segments.en: just like the process of training in AI,
segments.zh: 就像AI训练的过程一样，

segments.en: we're trained down to like remove the ones
segments.zh: 我们接受过训练，喜欢删除那些

segments.en: that aren't useful and build up on the ones that are.
segments.zh: 那些没有用的东西是建立在有用的东西的基础上的。

segments.en: Yeah, it's both pruning and strengthening.
segments.zh: 是的，既是修剪又是加固。

segments.en: And it seems like in humans,
segments.zh: 在人类看来，

segments.en: it's actually more pruning than strengthening,
segments.zh: 实际上更多的是修剪而不是加固，

segments.en: but it's both, and in AI is the same thing as both.
segments.zh: 但两者都是，而且在人工智能中两者是同一回事。

segments.en: So the first portion of training
segments.zh: 所以训练的第一部分

segments.en: is where they train the AI to predict text,
segments.zh: 是他们训练人工智能预测文本的地方，

segments.en: which is kind of like training it to read.
segments.zh: 这有点像训练它阅读。

segments.en: And it's a similar thing does happen in humans.
segments.zh: 类似的事情也发生在人类身上。

segments.en: So basically the random tangle gradually takes shape
segments.zh: 所以基本上随机的缠结逐渐形成

segments.en: and gradually sort of coalesces into more useful circuitry
segments.zh: 并逐渐合并成更有用的电路

segments.en: that has stored lots of facts about the world
segments.zh: 它存储了许多关于世界的事实

segments.en: and has stored lots of skills
segments.zh: 并且储存了很多技能

segments.en: for how to process information and transform it
segments.zh: 如何处理信息并进行转换

segments.en: and then produce predictions.
segments.zh: 然后产生预测。

segments.en: That's just the first step.
segments.zh: 这只是第一步。

segments.en: After they do the pre-training,
segments.zh: 当他们完成预训练后，

segments.en: then they try to teach it more useful skills
segments.zh: 然后他们尝试教它更有用的技能

segments.en: besides just predicting text.
segments.zh: 除了预测文本之外。

segments.en: And so, by the end of the process,
segments.zh: 所以，在这个过程结束时，

segments.en: they've thrown lots of coding problems at it.
segments.zh: 他们给它带来了很多编码问题。

segments.en: And they've said like, here's a coding problem.
segments.zh: 他们说，这是一个编码问题。

segments.en: Go. Here's a coding problem. Here's an environment.
segments.zh: 去。这里有一个编码问题。这里有一个环境。

segments.en: You have access to this virtual computer.
segments.zh: 您可以访问此虚拟计算机。

segments.en: Here's like the code base you're working with.
segments.zh: 这就像您正在使用的代码库。

segments.en: You can write code. You can edit the code.
segments.zh: 你可以写代码。您可以编辑代码。

segments.en: You can run the code. You can read it.
segments.zh: 您可以运行代码。你可以阅读它。

segments.en: You can use the internet. Go, go, go.
segments.zh: 您可以使用互联网。去！去！去。

segments.en: And it does that for a while.
segments.zh: 它会这样做一段时间。

segments.en: And then based on how successful it is, reinforcement happens.
segments.zh: 然后根据它的成功程度，进行强化。

segments.en: And they have thousands, maybe millions of examples of coding problems
segments.zh: 他们有数千甚至数百万个编码问题的例子

segments.en: like that that they trained it on.
